title: Umami Fluffy Egyptian Figs Stew
description: A comforting, savory stew that marries sweet, earthy figs with a silky, egg‑free texture, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat; add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Add minced garlic, ground cumin, and ground cinnamon, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for another 2 minutes to deglaze the pot.
Add the halved fresh figs and chopped dried figs, cooking for 2–3 minutes to soften.
Pour in the vegetable broth, tamari, miso paste, and coconut milk; stir well to combine all flavors.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and let it cook for 15 minutes so the figs absorb the umami broth.
Reduce the heat to low, add an extra splash of coconut milk if desired for extra fluffiness, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Use an immersion blender to partially blend the stew, creating a silky texture while keeping some whole figs for bite.
Simmer uncovered for another 5 minutes until the stew thickens to a creamy consistency.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with fresh mint or a drizzle of coconut milk for extra aroma.
